bug_fix_report.reproduction: After a cache cleanup, run test_reassignment_is_tenant_isolated with Tenant A warming the reassignment-policy cache before Tenant B requests project-42; Tenant B deterministically receives Tenant A's policy instead of its own.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: The reassignment-policy cache key contains only the project id and omits tenant identity, causing Tenant A and Tenant B requests for project-42 to share the same cache entry.
confirming_evidence: Logging only the computed cache key showed both tenant requests using reassignment-policy:project-42, and source inspection confirmed that the key contains only the project id.
Including tenant id in the cache key made the original cross-tenant reproduction pass for 50 consecutive paired requests and kept the surrounding policy tests passing.
Removing only the cache-key change caused the original cross-tenant failure to return on the first paired request.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Include tenant id in the reassignment-policy cache key while leaving the correctly functioning authorization code unchanged.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Retain test_reassignment_is_tenant_isolated and hand the verified boundary to pc-tdd.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The authorization guard was bypassed because the cleanup moved it.
debug_journal.prediction: The guard trace will be absent for Tenant B.
debug_journal.experiment: Add a trace at guard entry without changing code behavior.
debug_journal.result: The guard ran and authorized Tenant B correctly, so the hypothesis was rejected and no authorization patch was applied.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The cache key omits tenant identity.
debug_journal.prediction: The same cache entry id will appear for Tenant A and Tenant B.
debug_journal.experiment: Log only the computed cache key.
debug_journal.result: Both requests used reassignment-policy:project-42, and source inspection confirmed that the key contains only the project id.
